# Preface {.unnumbered}

This is a book about making good decisions when you cannot get the facts first, which is the work entrepreneurs actually do. It will not promise you certainty; no honest book could. It promises something more useful: that you can learn to decide well without it.

The method is a single repeatable path, five gates every decision passes through (Frame, Prior, Evidence, Sense, Call), from finding the real question to making the call and standing behind it. You will watch the whole path work once, on a real venture, before taking each gate in depth, and you will follow that venture through the entire book.

It is also a book for this moment, when every founder has an AI that can do a startling share of the analytical work. This book neither pretends the tool away nor hands it your judgment. Throughout, two things sit side by side: the reasoning you have to own, and the places to put the AI to work under your direction. A companion layer at the end speaks to the AI directly, so you can give it the mechanical work and keep the call.

You need no statistics and no algebra. The few formulas that appear are optional, set aside for the curious. What you need is a real decision you are facing. Bring one, and read with it in mind.
